meta.title: W176 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Mercedes W176 engine — OM607, OM651, M270, M133. Real UK costs, honest reliability data. Compare prices from 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes A-Class W176 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned W176 engine typically costs £2,200–£10,000+ supply only, depending on the engine — the volume M270.910 sits at £2,500–£4,000, while the M133 AMG unit ranges from £6,500–£10,000+. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes W176 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM651.901 diesel (A180 CDI/A200 CDI) and the M270.910 petrol (A180/A200) are the most dependable. The OM651 is a proven Mercedes-built diesel with a 150,000-mile+ lifespan, while the M270 petrol is smooth, robust, and free of major failure modes. The M133 AMG is reliable for a 360bhp+ unit but demands higher maintenance budgets.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes A-Class W176?

acceptedAnswer.text: Injector and EGR failure on the Renault-sourced OM607 diesel, VANOS and timing-chain wear on M270/M133 petrols, HPFP and turbo issues on the A45 AMG, and — unrelated to the engine — rear subframe corrosion on high-mileage cars.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes A-Class W176 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on the engine. The OM651 diesel and M270 petrol are genuinely robust and well-documented. The OM607 (Renault-sourced) is less refined and more prone to injector/EGR issues. The M133 is a high-strung performance engine — reliable if maintained, but expensive when it fails.

mainEntity.name: Which W176 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine outright — but the OM607.951 in the A160 CDI/A180 CDI is the one we'd advise against unless you're on a very tight budget. It feels underpowered in the W176's heavier body and its Renault-derived parts can be temperamental compared to Mercedes-built units.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my W176 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Rough idle or loss of power (OM607), VANOS rattle or chain noise (M270/M133), or long cranking and limp mode (M133 HPFP) are key warnings. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes A-Class W176 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well-kept £10,000+ W176 (especially an A250 or A45), a £3,500–£4,000 recon is 35–40% of the car's value — safe. Against a tired £4,000 example with a high-mileage OM607, a £3,000+ recon may exceed the car's worth — walk away. Our 70% Mercedes threshold is the line.

mainEntity.name: How long do Mercedes A-Class W176 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The OM651 diesel and M270 petrol can exceed 150,000 miles with proper servicing. The M133 AMG is typically good for 100,000+ miles if maintained to AMG standards, but its running costs are significantly higher. The OM607 is the shortest-lived due to its injector and EGR issues.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the W176 and other A-Class models / related cars?

acceptedAnswer.text: The W176 (2012–2018) is the 5-door hatchback that launched Mercedes' MFA platform. The W177 (2018–present) is the fourth-generation A-Class with a new MFA2 platform and updated engines. The CLA-Class (C117) and GLA-Class (X156) share the MFA platform and many engines but are distinct body styles with separate Generation Pages — costs and labour access vary.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes A-Class W176 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; the engine code is also stamped on the block. Tell us your variant (e.g., A200, A180 CDI, A45 AMG) and registration year, and we'll identify it — 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.h1: Mercedes A-Class W176 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the W176 hatchback — from the OM607 diesel to the M270 turbo petrols and the fire-breathing M133 in the A45 AMG. Real UK replacement costs and honest reliability data for Mercedes' first truly premium compact. Backed by 122+ real UK W176 enquiries in 2025 [MB-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: Mercedes A-Class W176 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the W176 hatchback specifically — from the entry-level OM607 diesel to the 360bhp M133 AMG unit that redefined the hot-hatch segment.

engines.reliability: ★★★☆☆ [PROVISIONAL — pending dedicated Engine Page; see Data Note]

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: OM651.901 and OM651.930: 80% confidence — the OM651 family is one of the most documented Mercedes diesel families in the UK. M270.910 and M270.920: 80% confidence — well-documented turbo-petrol family with a strong reliability record. M133.980: 75% confidence — AMG performance unit with higher running costs; reliability is good for a 360bhp+ four-cylinder but maintenance is expensive. OM607.951: 70% confidence — entry-level Renault-sourced diesel; less documented in the EM dataset but appears in the W176 parts catalogues.

overview.intro: The W176 is the third-generation A-Class and the first to sit on Mercedes' new MFA (Modular Front Architecture) platform, launched at the 2012 Geneva Motor Show. It marked a radical departure from the tall, sandwich-platform W168 and W169 predecessors — lower, wider, and unapologetically premium. The engine range spans Renault-sourced OM607 diesels, Mercedes' own OM651 diesels, the M270 turbo-petrol fours, and the M133 AMG unit that made the A45 the world's most powerful production four-cylinder at launch. A mid-life facelift in 2015 brought revised styling and the 381bhp A45.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: Mercedes MFA (shared with B-Class W246, CLA-Class C117) • Predecessor: W169 • Successor: W177 • Sibling models on this platform: B-Class (W246), CLA-Class (C117), GLA-Class (X156).
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The W176 A-Class generated 122 real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [MB-VERIFIED], concentrated on the M270 petrol fours and the OM651 diesel as these cars exit manufacturer warranty.

items.quote: The 1.8-litre OM651 is the sweet spot in the W176 diesel range — no timing chain tensioner issues, no injector seal 'Black Death' that plagued older CDI units, and a genuine 150,000-mile-plus lifespan with routine servicing. The 2.2-litre OM651.930 in the A220 d is equally robust.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want a diesel without the entry-level OM607's limitations.

items.quote: The 2.0-litre turbo M270 delivers 211bhp in a genuinely refined, responsive package. It's the engine that convinced the world Mercedes could do a proper premium hatchback — smooth, punchy, and without the M133's eye-watering running costs.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the performance of a hot hatch without the AMG tax.

items.quote: Renault-sourced 1.5-litre diesel. Adequate economy but feels underpowered in the W176's heavier body and lacks the OM651's refinement and durability. Injector and EGR issues are more common than on the Mercedes-built OM651.
items.whoItsFor: budget-conscious buyers prioritising fuel economy over everything else.

items.quote: When a 360–381bhp four-cylinder goes wrong, it goes wrong expensively. Turbocharger failure, high-pressure fuel pump issues, and the sheer labour intensity of working in the AMG engine bay mean replacement costs frequently hit five figures. The A45's M133 is a masterpiece — but it's a masterpiece with a maintenance bill to match.
items.whoItsFor: buyers with deep pockets and a full AMG service history.

items.quote: The 1.6-litre turbo M270 offers genuinely good economy, adequate power (122–156bhp), and the same fundamental robustness as its 2.0-litre sibling. It's the volume engine for good reason — cheap to run, cheap to replace, and more than enough for UK roads.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want a premium hatchback without the premium running costs.

items.quote: The 2.2-litre diesel is rare on UK roads compared to the volume OM651.901 and M270 models, but it offers 177bhp, all-wheel-drive traction, and genuine longevity. It's the W176's sleeper pick — a diesel that drives like a petrol.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who need diesel economy with four-wheel-drive capability.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Budget Diesel — economy-focused, but feels underpowered.
Best Diesel — the W176's most dependable oil-burner.
Rare Gem — AWD diesel, hard to find but worth it.
Best Petrol (Value) — reliable, cheap to run.
Most Expensive — AMG tax is real.

economicsRule.title: The W176 rule
economicsRule.text: A £2,800–£4,800 OM651 recon against a £6,000 W176 sits at 47–80% of the car's value. On a sub-£5,000 W176, that brushes our 70% Mercedes threshold — we'll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well-kept £10,000+ example (particularly an A45 AMG or A250), the same recon drops to a routine 28–48% — a repair decision, not a financial one. Value the car first.

problems.title: OM607.951 Injector & EGR Failure
problems.rootCause: The OM607 is a Renault K9K derivative — injector seal failure (carbon buildup, 'Black Death' style) and EGR valve clogging are common on high-mileage examples, particularly those used for short urban trips.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of rough idle or loss of power, get the injector seals and EGR inspected immediately — a £400–£800 repair is dramatically cheaper than the £2,200–£3,800 replacement that follows if ignored. On a W176 worth under £5,000, this decision alone determines whether the car stays economically viable.

cta.label: Compare OM607 engine prices for your W176 A180 CDI →

problems.title: M270/M133 VANOS & Timing Chain Wear
problems.affectedModels: All M270 and M133 petrol variants — A180, A200, A250, A45 AMG.
problems.rootCause: The M270 family's VANOS solenoids and timing chain tensioners can wear with age, particularly on cars with extended oil-change intervals. The M133's higher output accelerates this wear.
problems.recommendation: On any W176 petrol over 60,000 miles, have the VANOS solenoids and chain tensioner checked at every service — £500–£900 now is far cheaper than the £2,500–£4,000+ replacement that follows a skipped chain.

cta.label: Compare M270 engine prices for your W176 A250 →

problems.title: A45 AMG M133 Turbo & HPFP Failure
problems.rootCause: The M133's high-pressure fuel pump and turbocharger are pushed to their limits — 360–381bhp from 2.0 litres creates significant heat and stress. HPFP failure causes long cranking and limp mode; turbo failure is usually preceded by a whistling noise and loss of power.
problems.recommendation: If your A45 has no documented HPFP or turbo history, treat it as overdue preventative maintenance — £1,500–£2,500 now is far cheaper than the £6,500+ replacement that follows a high-speed failure. This is the single most important financial decision an A45 owner will make.

cta.label: Compare M133 engine prices for your W176 A45 AMG →

problems.title: W176 Rear Suspension & Subframe Corrosion
problems.rootCause: Not an engine failure, but a documented W176 weak point — rear axle carrier and subframe mount corrosion, particularly on UK cars exposed to salt and moisture.
problems.recommendation: Have the rear subframe inspected on any W176 over 80,000 miles — it's the single most common non-engine reason an otherwise sound W176 fails an MOT.

cta.label: Compare W176 rear subframe repair quotes →

replacementCosts.h2: W176 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition
replacementCosts.note: No Generation variance from the Model Page's baseline figures — W176 hatchback shares identical engine-bay access with its W246 B-Class sibling, so costs are inherited directly per the Authority Model, with no divergence note required. M133 costs reflect AMG-specific parts and specialist labour.

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are W176 5-door hatchback only. The related CLA-Class (C117), B-Class (W246), and GLA-Class (X156) share many of these engines but are covered on their own Generation Pages, as fitment and labour access vary by platform.
